This is scope creep, but by modifying `_remove_logs` to take a user, it seems like this function should also take a user. Adding a `user` parameter to `_remove_logs` acknowledges that the user account SSHing to the machine may not have privileges to delete the file, but it's also possible in that environment the SSH user may not be able to read the files either. 

For consistency this should eventually get a `user` option, but it's not required since it's far more likely the SSH user can at least read the logs.

The non-strict synchronous mode is "no writes will be lost due to automatic failover". The strict mode is "all writes are two node durable", meaning that a catastrophic loss of any one node (node, including storage, is gone and is not coming back) will not lead to lost writes.

The situation where it matters is when the sync standby goes down for a bit and while the standby is down and a new one hasn't been chosen the master completely explodes.

**ZLL->RX_FRAME_FILTER &= ~(ZLL_RX_FRAME_FILTER_ACK_FT_MASK);**
